prompts: In a lens imaging system, the focal point is where parallel light rays converge after passing through a lens. The image formation depends on the object's position relative to the lens and its focal point. For a converging (convex) lens, an object beyond the focal point forms a real, inverted image on the other side, while an object inside the focal point forms a virtual, upright image on the same side. In ray diagrams, key rays such as the parallel ray going through the focal point and the ray through the center help determine the image's nature, size, and position.
